commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an MCP-specific stdio env predicate that blocks inherited host config-pivot env keys while preserving selected explicit credential keys, with focused transport-config test coverage.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +9. Total +42 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main only drops isDangerousHostEnvVarName in toMcpEnvRecord, while host env tests show ANSIBLE_CONFIG and TF_CLI_CONFIG_FILE are inherited-only dangerous names. I did not run a live repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The protected maintainer label, compatibility-sensitive MCP env behavior, and missing contributor real behavior proof make this a human review/proof gate rather than an autofix lane.

target.title: fix(mcp): harden stdio env filtering
target.body:
## Summary

Harden stdio MCP server environment filtering so configured child-process env drops inherited execution/config pivot variables consistently with OpenClaw host process hardening.

## Changes

- Adds a dedicated MCP stdio env predicate that keeps existing always-dangerous host env blocking.
- Drops inherited child-process config pivots such as Ansible and Terraform config env keys before stdio MCP spawn.
- Preserves explicit MCP credential env keys, such as `GITHUB_TOKEN`, so configured authenticated MCP servers keep working.
- Expands `resolveMcpTransportConfig` coverage for the new dropped and preserved env behavior.

## Validation

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Notes

- No `CHANGELOG.md` update.
- Worklog appended locally to `USER.md`; not included in this PR.
- Refs NVIDIA tracking issue: https://github.com/NVIDIA-dev/openclaw-tracking/issues/745
target.author: eleqtrizit
